description: This figure presents a misorientation angle distribution histogram derived from Electron Backscatter Diffraction (EBSD) characterization of an Fe–8% Al low-density steel subjected to hot torsion testing. The plot displays boundary frequency as a function of misorientation angle (0–65°), with a critical threshold at 15° (indicated by a vertical red line) distinguishing Low Angle Boundaries (LABs) from High Angle Boundaries (HABs). The distribution reveals that LABs constitute 47% of the boundaries while HABs account for 53%, yielding an average misorientation angle of 23.7°. The curve exhibits a characteristic high-frequency peak at low angles (<5°) followed by a gradual decrease and relatively uniform distribution at higher angles, indicating a microstructure containing both recovered subgrains and recrystallized grains. This boundary character distribution provides quantitative evidence for dynamic recrystallization mechanisms operating in the ferritic steel during high-temperature deformation (900–1100°C), specifically supporting the analysis of Continuous Dynamic Recrystallization (CDRX) versus Discontinuous Dynamic Recrystallization (DDRX) based on the relative fractions of LABs and HABs.
